title: Conversation Mode
summary: Casual character messaging with group chats, schedules, statuses, autonomous replies, commands, and familiar chat controls.
audience: Users who want companion-style chats, quick conversations, or character group messages.
where: Home > Conversation, then the chat setup wizard and chat settings drawer.

title: Roleplay Mode
summary: Long-form story writing with character narration, sprites, backgrounds, lorebooks, trackers, and optional agents.
audience: Users who want immersive scenes and creative writing rather than quick texting.
where: Home > Roleplay, then the setup wizard and chat settings drawer.

title: Game Mode
summary: A visual-novel and RPG-style mode with an AI Game Master, party, map, journal, inventory, combat, and scene tools.
audience: Players who want the app to help run an adventure instead of only writing dialogue.
where: Home > Game, then the setup wizard and in-game control panel.

title: Characters
summary: Create, import, organize, and edit character cards used across Conversation, Roleplay, and Game chats.
audience: Users building or collecting characters to chat or roleplay with.
where: Title bar > Characters.

title: Personas
summary: Manage who you play as, including persona descriptions and avatars that chats can use for context.
audience: Users who want different identities or player characters for different chats.
where: Title bar > Personas, or chat settings for per-chat persona choices.

title: Lorebooks
summary: Store world info, setting details, rules, backstory, and keyword-triggered context that the model can recall when relevant.
audience: Users with worlds, settings, factions, or recurring facts too large for a character card.
where: Title bar > Lorebooks, then attach lorebooks in chat settings.

title: Prompt Presets
summary: Control prompt structure, sections, variables, and generation behavior for different chat styles.
audience: Power users tuning how models receive instructions and context.
where: Title bar > Presets, plus per-chat settings.

title: Connections
summary: Configure model providers, API keys, local endpoints, image providers, voice providers, and default connections.
audience: Users setting up Marinara or switching which model powers chats and agents.
where: Title bar > Connections.

title: Agents
summary: Optional helpers that can track world state, review lorebooks, create images, run custom behavior, and enrich generation.
audience: Users who want extra systems layered on top of their main chat.
where: Title bar > Agents, and each chat settings drawer.

title: Settings
summary: Global controls for appearance, chat behavior, imports, themes, extensions, backups, updates, and advanced runtime options.
audience: Everyone configuring how the app looks, behaves, imports data, or connects to runtimes.
where: Title bar > Settings.

title: Imports
summary: Bring in characters, personas, lorebooks, presets, SillyTavern data, and Marinara profile exports.
audience: Users migrating from another frontend or restoring existing Marinara data.
where: Settings > Import.

title: Bot Browser
summary: Browse downloadable character cards and related community resources from inside Marinara.
audience: Users looking for characters and resources to start with.
where: Title bar > Browser.

title: Professor Mari
summary: A dedicated assistant surface for app questions, codebase help, and guided changes separate from active chats.
audience: Users who want help understanding or customizing Marinara without interrupting a chat.
where: Mari avatar button in the title bar or mobile top bar.

title: Game Tutorial
summary: A first-game spotlight tutorial for the Game map, party, controls, and narration/input area.
audience: Game Mode users who need a quick orientation after setup.
where: Auto-opens on the first game, and can be reopened from the Game controls help button.

title: Slash Commands
summary: Type / in the chat input to see command autocomplete for actions such as help, dice rolls, reminders, guided replies, and impersonation.
audience: Users who prefer quick typed actions instead of hunting through menus.
where: Conversation and Roleplay chat input; type / to open autocomplete.

title: Text-to-Speech
summary: Configure voice providers and let chats, narration, or characters speak with assigned voices.
audience: Users who want voiced messages, narration, or more immersive scenes.
where: Settings and connection/provider configuration, with chat-specific voice options where available.

title: Image Generation
summary: Use image connections for selfies, sprites, backgrounds, NPC portraits, and Game scene illustrations.
audience: Users who want visual scenes and character art generated from chat context.
where: Connections, Settings, chat settings, Game controls, and relevant agents.

title: Trackers
summary: World, persona, character, quest, and custom trackers summarize structured state that can appear in Roleplay and Game surfaces.
audience: Users who want visible continuity and structured state during long scenes.
where: Enable tracker agents in chat settings, then use the tracker side panel or HUD surfaces.

title: Discord Mirror
summary: Mirror chat messages into a Discord channel through a webhook while keeping character names and narration labels readable.
audience: Users who want to archive or share chat output outside Marinara.
where: Per-chat settings drawer.

title: Prompt Overrides
summary: Customize selected prompt templates for specialized generation behavior without editing core source.
audience: Power users tuning advanced prompts for chat, images, and helper behavior.
where: Settings > Advanced.

title: Quick Replies
summary: Optional input actions for posting without generation, guiding a reply, or impersonating a character from the chat toolbar.
audience: Users who want faster alternate send actions during writing.
where: Settings > Advanced, then the chat input toolbar.

title: Chat Memory And Summaries
summary: Long chats can use recalled fragments and automatic summaries so relevant context survives beyond the latest messages.
audience: Users running long conversations or campaigns that need continuity.
where: Per-chat settings drawer and runtime summary controls.
